title: Seismic structure of the incoming oceanic Nazca plate offshore central Chile: P-wave velocity, density, and mineralogical changes
abstract: P-wave velocity structure of the incoming oceanic Nazca plate at the outer rise offshore central Chile (~33°S) is derived from wide-angle seismic OBH data. Velocity gradients and density anomalies in the oceanic crust and uppermost mantle reflect mineralogical changes due to serpentinization from seawater infiltration through bending-related normal faults. The results constrain the degree of lithospheric hydration prior to subduction.

location.description: Outer rise of incoming Nazca plate offshore central Chile (~33°S); OBH wide-angle velocity structure revealing serpentinization from bending faults

processing.summary: P-wave velocity model of the outer rise Nazca plate (~33°S) derived from OBH wide-angle seismic data. Velocity gradients and density anomalies indicate serpentinization of oceanic crust and uppermost mantle through bending fault pathways. Results quantify lithospheric hydration before subduction at central Chile.
